overview: This U/B deck leverages early disruption and card advantage to stabilize against aggressive strategies while scaling into a dominant late-game threat. By efficiently filling the graveyard with cheap spells and discard, the deck enables its namesake creature to grow into a massive, unblockable attacker. Consistent value generation ensures the pilot can outlast opponents in attrition battles.
win_conditions: Death's Shadow attacking for lethal damage
Grinding out card advantage with Flow State until the opponent runs out of resources
Using discard to strip the opponent's game plan while maintaining a scaled threat

keep_criteria: Hands containing at least one one-mana spell
Hands with early disruption like Thoughtseize
Hands that can reliably cast Death's Shadow by turn three
Keep any hand with a land and a discount spell (Thoughtseize, Inquisition, or similar) or a direct way to cast Death's Shadow (Flow State, Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual).
Keep hands with 2+ lands if they include discard or ritual acceleration.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking one-mana spells or cheap cantrips
Hands with only high-cost cards and no draw
Hands without interaction or graveyard synergy
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only high-cost threats, or hands with multiple rituals but no way to cast Shadow.

early_game: Play one-mana spells to build the graveyard and disrupt the opponent
Use Thoughtseize to remove key threats or combo pieces
Prioritize land drops to ensure smooth mana base
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize on a key answer or win condition.
If holding rituals, save them for turn 2.
Turn 2: Cast a ritual (Dark/Cabal/Flow State), then cast Death's Shadow.
If the opponent has early threats, use Thoughtseize to remove removal or keep hand clear.
Land sequencing: Start with a dual land (Underground Sea, Underground River, etc.) if possible, or a basic Swamp.
mid_game: Cast Death's Shadow once the graveyard is sufficiently populated
Use Flow State to refill the hand and maintain tempo
Trade efficiently to keep the board clear for the scaled threat
always keep open mana for counterspells or protection if running them, but prioritize casting Shadow.
Manage hand by using Thoughtseize to strip answers to Shadow or combo pieces.
Graveyard is a resource for rituals
Commit Shadow immediately if you can cast it for 2 mana and the board is clear or you have removal.
late_game: Repeatedly cast Flow State to generate overwhelming card advantage
Attack with Death's Shadow while using discard to strip remaining answers
Grind out the game by out-valuing the opponent's resource pool
Death's Shadow attacking for lethal, often supported by discard to clear blockers or rituals to cast it multiple times.
Flow State can tutor for Shadow or other threats to close out games.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to lethal, the opponent has no sweepers in hand, or you've established a board advantage that they cannot easily reset.

key_cards.name: Death's Shadow
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and scalable threat
key_cards.notes: Cast after filling the graveyard with cheap spells and discard to maximize power and toughness

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.role: Early game disruption and graveyard fuel
key_cards.notes: Use to remove opponent's key threats or combo pieces while simultaneously enabling Death's Shadow

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Cast to draw cards, refill the hand, and add more bodies to the graveyard for scaling

sequencing_tips: Hold Thoughtseize for the opponent's most impactful card rather than using it preemptively
Cast Flow State after the opponent commits resources to maximize value
Prioritize graveyard depth before casting Death's Shadow to ensure it enters the battlefield at a threatening size
Common mistake: overpaying for Shadow or failing to track opponent's life total.
Unique interaction: Flow State can tutor for Shadow or other threats, but be careful of graveyard hate.
Always calculate your life total carefully
a single damage spell can make Shadow uncastable.
In Unknown format, adapt to specific rulesets, but the core life-total math remains critical.

matchups.advice: Use Thoughtseize to remove key finishers and play cheap spells to scale Death's Shadow quickly. Prioritize survival until turn three.
Play patiently, using Flow State to outdraw them and discard to strip their interaction. Wait for them to use removal before committing Death's Shadow.
Race them by scaling Death's Shadow early and using discard to disrupt their value engines. Maintain board presence to pressure them.
Use discard to remove counterspells. Cast Shadow at end step or when they're tapped out. Use Flow State to tutor around counters.
Aggressive discard to hit their ramp spells. Use Thoughtseize to strip key mana rocks. Pressure them before they stabilize.
Win the discard war. Track their life total to know when they can cast Shadow. Use Thoughtseize to strip their rituals or Shadow. Prioritize keeping lands and discard spells.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath, Go for the Throat), hard removal (Path, Murder), and combo win conditions.
Note when they tap out for a threat vs. when they hold up mana for interaction.
Track their life total carefully, as it dictates Shadow's casting cost.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Death's Shadow scales with graveyard size
Flow State provides recurring card advantage

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Flow State timing optimizes card advantage
